title: Ayushman Bharat Yojana (PM-JAY)
department: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
overview: Ayushman Bharat National Health Protection Mission is a flagship scheme of Government of India to provide free access to healthcare for 50 crore citizens.
eligibility: Families listed under the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) database.
salary: Cashless health cover of Rs. 5 Lakhs per family per year.
how_to_apply: Check your name in the SECC list on the PM-JAY website or visit the nearest Ayushman Mitra at any empanelled hospital with an Aadhaar card or Ration card.
meta_title: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY: Free Rs. 5 Lakh Health Cover Scheme
meta_description: Apply online for Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY health card. Find eligibility rules, cashless benefits list, and how to download your golden card.
content:
### Key Scheme Benefits
- Cashless hospitalization at all empanelled public and private hospitals.
- Covers up to 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days of post-hospitalization expenses.
- All pre-existing conditions are covered from day one.

### Documents Required for Verification
* Aadhaar Card or Identity Proof
* Ration Card or SECC Letter
* Family Identity Document (if applicable)

title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
overview: PM-KISAN is a central sector scheme that provides financial assistance to all landholding farmer families across the country to supplement their financial needs.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families in India (subject to exclusion criteria like income tax payers).
salary: Direct income support of Rs. 6,000 per year paid in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000.
how_to_apply: Register through PM-Kisan portal (pmkisan.gov.in) via Self-Registration or through Common Service Centres (CSC).
meta_title: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi: Apply Online for Rs. 6000 Income Support
meta_description: Register online for PM Kisan Scheme. Check beneficiary status, eligibility list, installment schedules, and Aadhaar seeding requirements.
content:
### Financial Assistance Details
- Mode of payment: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into Bank Accounts.
- Frequency: Three equal installments of Rs. 2,000/- every 4 months.
- Coverage: Around 12 Crore farmer families across India.

### Documents Required to Register
* Aadhaar Card (Mandatory)
* Landholding Documents / Land Registry Papers
* Bank Account Passbook (with KYC verification)
* Mobile Number linked to Aadhaar Card

title: Kanyashree Prakalpa (West Bengal)
department: Department of Women and Child Development and Social Welfare, WB
overview: An award-winning initiative by the Government of West Bengal to improve the lives and status of girls by helping them continue education and delaying marriage until age 18.
eligibility: Unmarried girls resident of West Bengal aged 13-18 studying in classes VIII to XII (K1) and girls aged 18-19 pursuing higher education (K2).
salary: Annual scholarship of Rs. 1,000 (K1) and a one-time grant of Rs. 25,000 (K2).
how_to_apply: Apply through the institution/school where the girl is studying. Collect Form K1/K2, fill details, and submit with documents.
meta_title: WB Kanyashree Prakalpa Scheme: Eligibility & Apply Steps
meta_description: Apply for Kanyashree K1 annual Rs. 1000 scholarship and K2 Rs. 25000 grant. Find eligibility criteria, forms, and tracking rules for West Bengal.
content:
### Scheme Categories
- **K1 Scholarship**: Rs. 1,000/- annually for girls aged 13-18 enrolled in class VIII to XII (provided family income is under Rs. 1,20,000/yr, exempt for disabled).
- **K2 One-Time Grant**: Rs. 25,000/- one-time grant for girls on reaching 18 years, provided they are in education and unmarried.
- **K3 Scholarship**: For girls pursuing PG courses in Science, Arts, and Commerce.

### Documents Required
* Age Proof Certificate (Birth Certificate)
* Declaration that the applicant is unmarried
* Income Certificate (if family income limit applies)
* Institution Certificate of Enrollment
* Bank Account Details in candidate's name

title: Mukhyamantri Kanya Sumangala Yojana (Uttar Pradesh)
department: Women and Child Development Department, UP
overview: A state-sponsored conditional cash transfer scheme in Uttar Pradesh providing financial aid to girls at various stages from birth to graduation.
eligibility: Girl child born in a family resident of UP (max 2 girls per family), with annual family income under Rs. 3 Lakhs.
age_limit: From birth up to college age
salary: Total financial assistance of Rs. 15,000 paid in six phases.
how_to_apply: Apply online via the official MKSY portal (mksy.up.gov.in) or submit an offline application to the Block Development Officer (BDO).
meta_title: UP Kanya Sumangala Yojana: Scheme Details & Apply Online
meta_description: Apply for UP Kanya Sumangala Scheme. Get financial support of Rs. 15,000 in six stages. View eligibility, documents, and application status.
content:
### Benefit Distribution Stages
- **Stage 1**: Rs. 2,000/- at birth.
- **Stage 2**: Rs. 1,000/- on complete vaccination by age 1.
- **Stage 3**: Rs. 2,000/- on admission to Class 1.
- **Stage 4**: Rs. 2,000/- on admission to Class 6.
- **Stage 5**: Rs. 3,000/- on admission to Class 9.
- **Stage 6**: Rs. 5,000/- on admission to Bachelor's Degree or Diploma Course.

### Documents Required
* Address Proof of UP (Domicile Certificate)
* Family Income Certificate (under Rs. 3 Lakh/yr)
* Joint photo of applicant girl with parent
* Birth Certificate of the girl child
* Bank Account Details of parent (or girl if major)

title: Mukhyamantri Ladli Behna Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
department: Women and Child Development Department, MP
overview: A landmark direct cash transfer scheme in Madhya Pradesh to foster economic independence among women and improve their health and nutrition levels.
eligibility: Married women (including widows, divorcees) resident of MP, aged 21 to 60, whose family income is under Rs. 2.5 Lakhs/year.
salary: Direct monthly financial aid of Rs. 1,250 transferred directly into bank accounts.
how_to_apply: Apply through camps organized in local Gram Panchayats, wards, or Anganwadi centres by submitting the application form with biometric e-KYC.
meta_title: MP Ladli Behna Yojana: Rs. 1250 Monthly Scheme for Women
meta_description: Complete details on MP Chief Minister Ladli Behna Scheme. Check payment status, registration forms, beneficiary lists, and e-KYC guidelines.
content:
### Key Benefits
- Direct cash transfer of Rs. 1,250/- per month (Rs. 15,000/- annually).
- Empowers rural and urban women with financial autonomy.
- Enhances household nutrition and children's education spending.

### Exclusion Criteria
* Family income exceeds Rs. 2.5 Lakhs per year.
* Family members pay income tax.
* Any family member owns a four-wheeler.
* Family members hold government jobs.

title: Rythu Bandhu Investment Support Scheme (Telangana)
department: Agriculture Department, Government of Telangana
overview: A farmers' investment support scheme of Telangana government that provides grant-in-aid to landowning farmers at the start of Kharif and Rabi seasons.
eligibility: Landowning farmers in Telangana holding agriculture land.
salary: Financial assistance of Rs. 10,000 per acre per year.
how_to_apply: No separate application required for existing landholders. New landholders must submit Pattadar Passbook details to the local Agriculture Extension Officer (AEO).
meta_title: Telangana Rythu Bandhu Scheme: Investment Support for Farmers
meta_description: Get details on Telangana Rythu Bandhu. View seasonal direct payouts of Rs. 5000 per acre, Pattadar passbook registration, and land status check.
content:
### Scheme Highlights
- The first direct-investment support scheme for farmers in India.
- Helps farmers purchase inputs like seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, and labor.
- Coined as a model scheme for the central PM-KISAN program.

### Documents Required (for new farmers)
* Pattadar Dharani Passbook
* Aadhaar Card Copy
* Bank Account Passbook Copy linked to Aadhaar

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
department: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs & Ministry of Rural Development
overview: PMAY is a flagship housing mission of the Government of India that aims to provide 'Housing for All' in rural (PMAY-G) and urban (PMAY-U) areas.
eligibility: Families belonging to Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Groups (MIG) who do not own a pucca house.
salary: Up to Rs. 2.67 Lakhs interest subsidy on home loans.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the PMAY portal or visit a local Common Service Centre (CSC) to submit details.
meta_title: PMAY Housing Scheme: Apply Online for Home Loan Subsidy
meta_description: Register online for Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban & Gramin). Check credit-linked subsidy eligibility, lists, and subsidy calculators.
content:
### Scheme Components
- **In-situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR)**: Using land as resource with private participation.
- **Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS)**: Interest subsidy up to 6.5% on home loans.
- **Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP)**: Government financial aid of Rs. 1.5 Lakh per EWS house.
- **Beneficiary-led Construction (BLC)**: Assistance up to Rs. 1.5 Lakh for house construction.

### Documents Required
* Identity Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID)
* Address Proof
* Income Certificate / Salary Slip / Form 16
* Land Ownership Proof (for construction)
* Affidavit stating that no family member owns a pucca house in India

title: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) Account
department: Ministry of Women and Child Development
overview: SSY is a small deposit scheme of the Government of India launched under 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign, offering high interest rates for a girl child's education and marriage.
eligibility: Parent or legal guardian of a girl child resident of India, aged below 10 years (max 2 accounts per family).
salary: High compound interest rate (8.2% p.a.) with tax benefits under Section 80C.
how_to_apply: Open the account at any post office or authorized commercial bank branch by filling the SSY Application Form.
meta_title: Sukanya Samriddhi Account: High-Interest Scheme for Girl Child
meta_description: Open Sukanya Samriddhi account at Post Office or Bank. Check current 8.2% interest rates, calculator, tax exemptions, and maturity guidelines.
content:
### Account Operations
- **Minimum Deposit**: Rs. 250/- per year.
- **Maximum Deposit**: Rs. 1,500,000/- per year.
- **Maturity Period**: 21 years from account opening date or until girl's marriage after age 18.
- **Withdrawal Rules**: 50% withdrawal allowed for higher education after the girl reaches 18 years of age.

### Documents Required
* SSY Account Opening Form
* Birth Certificate of the Girl Child (Mandatory)
* ID and Address Proof of the Parent/Guardian (Aadhaar, PAN)
* Passport size photographs of Parent and Child

title: West Bengal Annapurna Bhandar Scheme 2026
department: Department of Women & Child Development and Social Welfare, West Bengal
overview: The Government of West Bengal has launched the Annapurna Bhandar Scheme, effective from June 1, 2026. This landmark initiative replaces the previous Lakshmir Bhandar scheme, providing enhanced financial assistance of ₹3,000 per month directly to eligible women across the state to promote financial independence and support family livelihood.
eligibility: Female permanent residents of West Bengal, aged 25 to 60 years. Government employees, pensioners, teaching/non-teaching staff in government-aided institutions, and income taxpayers are excluded.
salary: ₹3,000 per month (transferred directly via DBT)
how_to_apply:
1. Fresh applicants must register on the official Annapurna Bhandar online portal (launching June 1, 2026) or visit Duare Sarkar camps.
2. Fill in the online/offline application form with personal, Aadhaar, and bank details.
3. Upload required documents: Aadhaar card, proof of West Bengal residence, bank passbook photocopy, and passport-size photo.
4. Submit the form. Existing Lakshmir Bhandar beneficiaries will be automatically migrated subject to eligibility verification.
meta_title: West Bengal Annapurna Bhandar Scheme 2026: Form, Eligibility & Status
meta_description: Apply online for West Bengal Annapurna Bhandar Scheme 2026. Get financial benefits of ₹3000/month. Check eligibility, required documents, and apply steps.
content:
# West Bengal Annapurna Bhandar (Yojana) Scheme 2026

The West Bengal Government has introduced the **Annapurna Bhandar Scheme** (officially referred to as **Annapurna Yojana** on the social security portal) as a successor to the Lakshmir Bhandar scheme. Starting **June 1, 2026**, eligible women in West Bengal will receive **₹3,000 per month** directly in their bank accounts.

### Benefits & Monthly Assistance
- **Standard Assistance**: ₹3,000 per month for all eligible women.
- **Payment Mode**: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the beneficiary's linked bank account.

### Eligibility Criteria
- **Gender**: Only female applicants are eligible.
- **Residency**: Must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
- **Age Limit**: Between **25 and 60 years** as of the application date.
- **Exclusion Criteria**:
  - Current or retired government employees (receiving salary or pension).
  - Employees of statutory bodies, municipalities, panchayats, or government-aided educational institutions.
  - Income taxpayers.

### Migration from Lakshmir Bhandar
- Existing beneficiaries of the Lakshmir Bhandar scheme will be automatically migrated to the new Annapurna Bhandar Scheme.
- Beneficiaries must undergo verification to ensure they do not fall under any of the exclusion criteria.

### Documents Required for Application
- Aadhaar Card
- West Bengal Resident Domicile Proof
- Bank Account Passbook (with IFSC and Account Number)
- Caste Certificate (if applicable)
- Passport Size Photograph
- Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

### How to Apply Online
1. Visit the official West Bengal State Portal or the dedicated Annapurna Bhandar portal (launching June 1, 2026).
2. Click on the **Apply Online for Annapurna Bhandar** link.
3. Enter your active mobile number to receive an OTP.
4. Fill in the application form with personal details, Aadhaar number, and Swasthya Sathi card number.
5. Provide bank account details carefully for DBT transfers.
6. Upload the scanned copies of required documents.
7. Submit the application and print the acknowledgement slip for status tracking.

### Official Guidelines & Application Form Links

Download the official **Annapurna Yojana Family Level Data Collection Form** in your preferred language:

* **English Application Form**: [Download PDF Form (English)](https://socialsecurity.wb.gov.in/public/pdf/Annapurna_Yojana_Family_Level_Data_Collection_Form_English.pdf)
* **Bangla (Bengali) Application Form**: [Download PDF Form (Bengali)](https://socialsecurity.wb.gov.in/public/pdf/Annapurna_Yojana_Family_Level_Data_Collection_Form_Bangla.pdf)
* **Hindi Application Form**: [Download PDF Form (Hindi)](https://socialsecurity.wb.gov.in/public/pdf/Annapurna_Yojana_Family_Level_Data_Collection_Form_Hindi.pdf)

* **Official WB Social Security Portal**: [socialsecurity.wb.gov.in](https://socialsecurity.wb.gov.in)

title: Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana 2026
department: Department of Women and Child Development, Maharashtra
overview: The Government of Maharashtra operates the Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana to support women. The scheme provides ₹1,500 per month directly to women aged 21-65 to ensure their economic independence, health, and nutrition status.
eligibility: Married, divorced, widowed, or single women who are permanent residents of Maharashtra, aged 21 to 65 years. The annual family income must be under ₹2.5 Lakh.
salary: ₹1,500 per month
how_to_apply:
1. Download the Nari Shakti Doot mobile app or visit the official online portal at ladkibahin.maharashtra.gov.in.
2. Register using mobile number and Aadhaar.
3. Fill the application form online.
4. Upload Domicile certificate (or 15-year residence proof), Aadhaar, Bank Passbook, and Income Certificate.
5. Submit for verification by local authorities.
meta_title: Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana: Form, Status & Link
meta_description: Apply online for Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana. Check eligibility, download application form, required documents and status tracking.
content:
# Maharashtra Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana 2026

The Government of Maharashtra has implemented the **Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana** to provide direct financial assistance of **₹1,500 per month** to women across the state.

### Scheme Objectives
- Provide monthly financial aid to ensure self-reliance among women.
- Support nutrition and health care needs of underprivileged families.

### Detailed Eligibility Guidelines
- **Residency**: Must be a resident of Maharashtra (Domicile certificate or school leaving certificate showing place of birth in Maharashtra required).
- **Age Bracket**: Women aged **21 to 65 years**.
- **Family Income**: Total annual income of the family must not exceed **₹2.5 Lakh**.
- **Bank Account**: Beneficiary must have a bank account linked with Aadhaar.

### Excluded Candidates
- Families with an active income taxpayer.
- Families where any member is a permanent employee or pensioner of a government department or statutory body.
- Families owning a four-wheeler (except tractors).

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar Card
- Domicile Certificate or Birth Certificate of Maharashtra
- Income Certificate (proving annual income < ₹2.5 Lakh)
- Bank Account Passbook copy
- Passport size photograph
- Signed self-declaration form

### Application Procedure
1. Visit the portal [ladkibahin.maharashtra.gov.in](https://ladkibahin.maharashtra.gov.in) or download the **Nari Shakti Doot** application.
2. Sign up and log in using your credentials.
3. Select the scheme application form.
4. Fill in basic details, bank details, and upload documents.
5. Submit the application form and keep the receipt number.

title: Bihar Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana 2026
department: Social Welfare Department & Education Department, Bihar
overview: The Bihar Government offers the Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana, providing financial support up to ₹50,000 in various stages to girls from birth through graduation. The scheme aims to prevent child marriage, promote female education, and decrease female infanticide.
eligibility: Girls who are permanent residents of Bihar. The benefit is limited to two girls per family.
salary: Up to ₹50,000 (Installments: ₹2,000 at birth, ₹1,000 at immunization, ₹10,000 at Class 10/12, and ₹50,000 upon graduation)
how_to_apply:
1. Apply online via the Medhasoft portal (medhasoft.bih.nic.in) or the official Kanya Utthan portal.
2. Registrations are done stage-wise (for school-level vs graduation-level).
3. Fill in student registration number, marks details, Aadhaar, and bank account.
4. Upload graduation marksheet, residential proof, and photo.
5. Verify and submit.
meta_title: Bihar Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana: Apply Online, Form & Status
meta_description: Apply online for Bihar Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana. Get details on registration, installment disbursals, documents required, and check application status.
content:
# Bihar Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana 2026

The **Mukhyamantri Kanya Utthan Yojana (MKUY)** is a flagship scheme by the Bihar Government that provides financial assistance of up to **₹50,000** to female students to promote higher education and check child marriages.

### Installment Disbursal Stages & Amount
- **At Birth**: ₹2,000
- **Upon 1-Year Complete Immunization**: ₹1,000
- **On Passing Class 12th (Unmarried Girls)**: ₹25,000
- **On Graduating (Married or Unmarried)**: ₹50,000

### Key Eligibility Terms
- Must be a permanent resident of Bihar.
- Must belong to a family with a maximum of two daughters.
- For school/graduation incentives, the student must pass the respective exams from a recognized board or university in Bihar.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar Card of student/parents
- Bihar Domicile Certificate
- Bank Passbook (linked with student's Aadhaar)
- Class 12th or Graduation Marksheet & Registration details
- Unmarried Certificate (signed by ward member/gazetted officer, only for intermediate pass incentive)

### How to Register Online
1. Visit the official online portal at [edudbt.bih.nic.in](http://edudbt.bih.nic.in) or Medhasoft portal.
2. Select the link corresponding to your passing year and stage (e.g. Graduation or Intermediate).
3. Register by entering university registration number and marks.
4. Fill in bank details, Aadhaar card number, and mobile number.
5. Log in with the received user ID and password to upload documents.
6. Verify all details and click on **Finalize Application**.

title: UP BC Sakhi Yojana 2026: Online Form & Details
department: Uttar Pradesh State Rural Livelihoods Mission (UPSRLM)
overview: The UP Government runs the BC Sakhi Scheme to deploy women Banking Correspondents in rural areas. It provides financial inclusion services to villagers at their doorstep and provides rural women with employment, earning opportunities, and training.
eligibility: Women permanent residents of Uttar Pradesh, aged 18 to 50 years, living in the same gram panchayat where applying. Basic understanding of banking operations and class 10 pass preferred.
salary: Monthly stipend of ₹4,000 for the first 6 months, plus transaction commissions.
how_to_apply:
1. Download the UP BC Sakhi mobile app from Google Play Store.
2. Register using mobile number and enter OTP.
3. Fill in personal profile, educational details, and experience.
4. Answer basic aptitude questions on banking and mathematics.
5. Select your gram panchayat and submit application.
meta_title: UP BC Sakhi Scheme 2026: Registration App, Stipend & Eligibility
meta_description: Apply for UP Banking Correspondent Sakhi Scheme 2026. Doorstep rural banking jobs. Get details on stipend, requirements, mobile app registration.
content:
# Uttar Pradesh BC Sakhi Scheme 2026

The **UP Banking Correspondent (BC) Sakhi Yojana** is an initiative by the Uttar Pradesh Government to empower rural women by providing them employment as doorstep banking correspondents.

### Role of a BC Sakhi
- Doorstep withdrawal, deposit, and transfer services for villagers.
- Opening savings accounts and promoting digital banking in rural areas.
- Assisting in the disbursement of other government schemes like PM-KISAN, pension payouts, etc.

### Benefits & Stipend Details
- **Stipend**: ₹4,000 per month for the initial 6 months.
- **Equipment Loan**: An interest-free loan of up to **₹75,000** to buy a laptop, POS machine, and setup equipment.
- **Transaction Commission**: Continuous earnings based on the commission of deposit and withdrawal transactions.

### Who can Apply?
- Females residing in the specific Gram Panchayat they are applying for.
- Minimum qualification of Class 10th pass.
- Age between **18 and 50 years**.
- Basic understanding of running mobile apps and calculators.

### Application Procedure
1. Search and install the **UP BC Sakhi** app from the Google Play Store on your smartphone.
2. Enter your mobile number to generate an OTP and register.
3. Complete the step-by-step profile section.
4. Upload a copy of your Class 10 certificate and passport-size photo.
5. Submit the questionnaire and keep a screenshot of the submission confirmation.

title: Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana 2026
department: Department of Medical Health & Family Welfare, Rajasthan
overview: The Rajasthan Government's Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana offers cashless health insurance coverage up to ₹25 Lakh per family per year. This scheme covers both general and critical illnesses, including organ transplants, at government and empaneled private hospitals.
eligibility: All families residing in Rajasthan. Free registration for NFSA cardholders, SECC category, small/marginal farmers, and contract workers; others pay a nominal annual premium of ₹850.
salary: Cashless health insurance cover up to ₹25 Lakh per family per year, and ₹5 Lakh accident cover.
how_to_apply:
1. Apply online through Rajasthan SSO Portal (sso.rajasthan.gov.in) using Jan Aadhaar card.
2. Log in, select 'Chiranjeevi Yojana' app.
3. Verify Jan Aadhaar family details.
4. Complete premium payment (if applicable) or select free registration category.
5. Print policy document.
meta_title: Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana 2026
meta_description: Apply for Rajasthan Chiranjeevi Health Insurance Scheme 2026. Cashless coverage up to ₹25 Lakh for all families. Check premium, hospital list, registry steps.
content:
# Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana 2026

The **Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana** is Rajasthan's flagship health insurance scheme that guarantees cashless medical treatment of up to **₹25 Lakh** per year for all families in the state.

### Key Scheme Benefits
- Cashless medical treatment in registered government and private hospitals.
- Coverage of pre-hospitalization and post-hospitalization medical expenses.
- Package coverage for over 1,700 medical procedures, including organ transplants, cancer treatment, and heart surgeries.
- Accidental Insurance cover of up to **₹5 Lakh** under the linked Chiranjeevi Durghatna Bima Yojana.

### Premium Structure
- **Free Category**: Small and marginal farmers, contract workers (Samvida Karmi), NFSA beneficiaries, SECC 2011 families, and destitute families.
- **Paid Category**: Families not falling in the above categories can register by paying a nominal annual premium of **₹850**.

### Registration Steps
1. Ensure your family has a **Jan Aadhaar Card** (mandatory for registration).
2. Visit the SSO Rajasthan portal at [sso.rajasthan.gov.in](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in).
3. Log in with your SSOID.
4. Search and select the **Chiranjeevi Bima Yojana** application.
5. Verify details using your Jan Aadhaar number.
6. Submit premium payment of ₹850 online if in the paid category.
7. Download and print the policy certificate.

title: Odisha Subhadra Yojana 2026
department: Women and Child Development Department, Odisha
overview: The Government of Odisha has launched the Subhadra Yojana, a flagship welfare initiative providing financial assistance of ₹50,000 over five years (₹10,000 annually in two installments of ₹5,000) directly to eligible women to support financial independence.
eligibility: Female permanent residents of Odisha, aged 21 to 60 years. Applicants must be covered under NFSA/SFSS or have a family income under ₹2.5 Lakh. Income taxpayers, government employees, and representatives are excluded.
salary: ₹50,000 over 5 years (₹10,000 annually)
how_to_apply:
1. Apply online via the official portal at subhadra.odisha.gov.in or offline by obtaining forms at designated Anganwadi Centres or Mo Seba Kendras.
2. Fill out the application form with personal, Aadhaar, and bank details.
3. Submit required documents: Aadhaar card, residence proof, bank details (linked with Aadhaar), and passport photo.
4. Complete e-KYC verification for approval.
meta_title: Odisha Subhadra Yojana 2026: Apply Online, Form & Status
meta_description: Apply for Odisha Subhadra Yojana 2026. Get ₹50,000 cash assistance over 5 years. Check eligibility, download form, and track application status.
content:
# Odisha Subhadra Yojana 2026

The Government of Odisha has introduced the **Subhadra Yojana** as a flagship women's welfare initiative. The scheme provides a total financial assistance of **₹50,000** over five years to eligible women in the state.

### Payment Structure & Installments
- **Total Assistance**: ₹50,000 over 5 years.
- **Annual Benefit**: ₹10,000 per year, paid in two equal installments of **₹5,000** each (usually on Raksha Bandhan and International Women's Day).
- **Payment Method**: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to Aadhaar-enabled bank accounts.

### Eligibility Criteria
- **Residency**: Must be a permanent resident of Odisha.
- **Gender**: Limited to female applicants.
- **Age Limit**: Must be between **21 and 60 years** old.
- **Financial Eligibility**: Must be covered under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) or State Food Security Scheme (SFSS). Non-ration card holders are eligible if their family income is under **₹2,50,000** per year.

### Exclusion Rules
- Income Tax payers.
- Government employees, regular or contractual, and government pensioners.
- Members of Parliament (MP), Members of Legislative Assembly (MLA).
- Anyone already receiving ₹1,500/month or ₹18,000/year under any other government scheme.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar Card (linked with mobile number)
- Bank Account Passbook (Aadhaar linked and enabled for DBT)
- Residence/Domicile Certificate
- Income Certificate (if not an NFSA/SFSS cardholder)
- Passport size photograph

### Application Process
1. **Online Method**:
   - Go to [subhadra.odisha.gov.in](https://subhadra.odisha.gov.in).
   - Click on the apply link and register using your mobile number.
   - Enter your Aadhaar details and fill in the form.
   - Verify details and submit.
2. **Offline Method**:
   - Get the free application form from your local Anganwadi Centre, Mo Seba Kendra, or Block Office.
   - Fill in details and attach copy of Aadhaar card and Bank passbook.
   - Submit at any Mo Seba Kendra or Common Service Centre (CSC) for free entry.

title: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana 2026
department: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), Central Government
overview: The Central Government of India operates the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana, offering free electricity up to 300 units per month through rooftop solar systems. The scheme provides massive solar installation subsidies up to ₹78,000 for residential households.
eligibility: All Indian citizens owning a residential house with a roof suitable for solar panels, with a functional electricity connection and no prior rooftop solar subsidies.
salary: Up to 300 units of free electricity per month, plus rooftop solar installation subsidy of up to ₹78,000.
how_to_apply:
1. Register on the official portal pmsuryaghar.gov.in using consumer number, state, and DISCOM.
2. Log in and submit the Rooftop Solar application form.
3. Wait for local DISCOM feasibility approval.
4. Hire an empanelled vendor to install the system.
5. Apply for net metering and complete commissioning.
6. Enter bank details and cancelled cheque to receive subsidy.
meta_title: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana 2026 | Apply Online & Subsidy
meta_description: Register online for PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana 2026. Get up to 300 units of free electricity and solar panel installation subsidies up to ₹78,000.
content:
# PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana 2026

The **PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana** is a central government initiative to provide up to **300 units of free electricity** per month to 1 crore households in India by installing rooftop solar systems.

### Solar Installation Subsidies
The government provides direct financial assistance (subsidy) based on the capacity of the rooftop solar system:
- **1 kW System**: ₹30,000 subsidy (Suitable for small houses using 0-150 units/month)
- **2 kW System**: ₹60,000 subsidy (Suitable for medium houses using 150-300 units/month)
- **3 kW System or more**: Maximum subsidy of **₹78,000** (Suitable for larger houses using over 300 units/month)

### Eligibility Criteria
- Must be an Indian citizen.
- Must own a residential property with a clear, shadow-free roof suitable for solar panels.
- Must have an active electricity connection with a local DISCOM (Electricity Distribution Company).
- Must not have availed of any other solar subsidy from the central or state government.

### Step-by-Step Online Application Guide
1. **Registration**:
   - Visit the official portal [pmsuryaghar.gov.in](https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in).
   - Enter your State, DISCOM name, Consumer Account Number (from electricity bill), Mobile Number, and Email.
2. **Apply for Rooftop Solar**:
   - Log in using your Consumer Number and Mobile OTP.
   - Fill in the application form and submit.
3. **DISCOM Feasibility Approval**:
   - The local DISCOM will inspect your connection and approve feasibility.
4. **Installation**:
   - Once approved, choose a registered, empanelled solar installer (vendor list available on portal) to install the rooftop panels.
5. **Net Metering**:
   - Submit net metering request on the portal after installation. DISCOM will install the net meter.
6. **Generate Commissioning Certificate**:
   - DISCOM will inspect and generate the commissioning certificate on the portal.
7. **Subsidy Payout**:
   - Enter your bank details (and upload a cancelled cheque copy). The subsidy will be credited to your bank account within 30 days!

title: Karnataka Gruha Lakshmi Scheme 2026
department: Department of Women and Child Development, Karnataka
overview: The Karnataka Government offers the Gruha Lakshmi Scheme, providing a monthly cash incentive of ₹2,000 directly to women heads of eligible households. The scheme aims to support livelihood, manage household expenditures, and empower housewives.
eligibility: Women identified as the head of the family in APL/BPL/Antyodaya Ration Cards. The woman or her husband must not be an income tax payer or GST registrant.
salary: ₹2,000 per month
how_to_apply:
1. Apply online via the Seva Sindhu Guarantee Schemes portal at sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/gruhalakshmi.
2. Enter Ration Card number, Aadhaar number, and mobile number linked with Aadhaar.
3. Provide Aadhaar-seeded bank account details for direct transfer.
4. Submit online or physically at Grama One, Bangalore One, Karnataka One, Nada Kacheris, or Bapuji Seva Kendra centers.
meta_title: Karnataka Gruha Lakshmi Scheme 2026: Apply Online & Check Status
meta_description: Apply online for Karnataka Gruha Lakshmi Scheme 2026. Get ₹2,000 monthly benefit for family women heads. Check list, eligibility, Seva Sindhu link.
content:
# Karnataka Gruha Lakshmi Scheme 2026

The **Gruha Lakshmi Scheme** is one of the key guarantee schemes implemented by the Karnataka Government, providing financial assistance of **₹2,000 per month** to the female head of households.

### Benefit & Payment
- **Monthly Aid**: ₹2,000 per month.
- **Transfer Method**: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the bank account linked with the beneficiary's Aadhaar.

### Eligibility Criteria
- The applicant must be a woman designated as the "Head of the Family" on BPL, APL, or Antyodaya ration cards.
- **Tax Exclusion**: The applicant woman or her husband must not be an Income Tax payer or a GST registrant.
- Only one woman per family (designated head on ration card) can receive the benefit.

### Documents Required for Application
- Ration Card (BPL/APL/Antyodaya Card)
- Aadhaar Card of the applicant woman
- Aadhaar Card of the husband
- Bank Passbook copy (linked to Aadhaar for DBT)
- Mobile number linked with Aadhaar

### How to Apply
1. **Online (Seva Sindhu Portal)**:
   - Visit the official Seva Sindhu Guarantee portal: [sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/gruhalakshmi](https://sevasindhugs.karnataka.gov.in/gruhalakshmi).
   - Enter your ration card number.
   - Enter OTP received on the registered mobile number.
   - Verify Aadhaar and bank details and submit.
2. **Offline Centers**:
   - Visit the nearest **Grama One**, **Karnataka One**, **Bangalore One**, **Bapuji Seva Kendra**, or **Nada Kacheri** center.
   - Submit your ration card, Aadhaar cards, and bank account copy to the operator.
   - The operator will register you for free and provide an acknowledgement printout.

title: West Bengal Rupashree Prakalpa 2026
department: Department of Women & Child Development and Social Welfare, West Bengal
overview: The West Bengal Government operates the Rupashree Prakalpa, offering a one-time financial grant of ₹25,000 to economically stressed families at the time of their adult daughter's marriage. The scheme helps parents meet marriage expenses without taking high-interest loans.
eligibility: Unmarried women who are permanent residents of West Bengal, aged 18 or above, whose family income is under ₹1.5 Lakh. The groom must be at least 21 years old, and it must be the applicant's first marriage.
age_limit: 18 Years and above (Groom must be 21 Years and above)
salary: ₹25,000 one-time grant
how_to_apply:
1. Obtain the application form from the Block Development Officer (BDO) office, Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) office, or Municipality office.
2. Submit the form 30 to 60 days before the marriage date.
3. Attach proof of age, family income, West Bengal residence, bank passbook, and proof of marriage (invitation card, marriage registrar notice, etc.).
meta_title: West Bengal Rupashree Prakalpa 2026: Form, Status & Eligibility
meta_description: Apply for West Bengal Rupashree Prakalpa 2026. Get one-time ₹25,000 financial support for daughter's wedding. Download application form & check status.
content:
# West Bengal Rupashree Prakalpa 2026

The **Rupashree Prakalpa** is a welfare scheme by the Government of West Bengal providing a one-time cash grant of **₹25,000** to daughters of poor families during their marriage.

### Key Benefits
- A one-time financial assistance of **₹25,000** credited directly to the applicant's bank account before the marriage.

### Detailed Eligibility Criteria
- **Age**: The bride must be at least **18 years** old and the groom must be at least **21 years** old on the date of marriage.
- **Marital Status**: The applicant must be unmarried and it must be her first marriage.
- **Residency**: The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
- **Income Limit**: Total family annual income must not exceed **₹1,50,000**.
- **Bank Account**: The applicant bride must have a personal bank account (joint accounts are not allowed).

### Required Documents
- Proof of Age of bride & groom (Birth Certificate, Madhyamik Admit Card, Voter ID, Aadhaar, or PAN card).
- Never-Married self-declaration.
- Income Certificate (issued by BDO, SDO, MP, MLA, or Gazetted Officer).
- Proof of West Bengal Residence (Voter Card, Ration Card, Domicile certificate).
- Bank Passbook front page copy showing IFSC and Account Number.
- Proof of proposed marriage (Wedding Card, Marriage Registrar Certificate, or declaration).
- Passport-size photos of both bride and groom.

### Application Process
1. **Download Form**: Collect the application form from your local BDO (for rural areas), SDO (for subdivisions), or Municipal Corporation office. Form is also available on [wbrupashree.gov.in](http://www.wbrupashree.gov.in).
2. **Timing**: Submit the filled form **30 to 60 days before the wedding date**.
3. **Submission**: Submit the completed form along with all supporting documents to the local BDO, SDO, or Municipality office.
4. **Verification**: An inquiry officer will conduct physical verification of the marriage and family income before sanctioning the grant.

title: Bihar Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana 2026
department: Department of Industries, Bihar
overview: The Bihar Government operates the Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana to promote startup culture and industrial growth. The scheme offers a financial assistance of up to ₹10 Lakh (50% interest-free loan, 50% subsidy/grant) to youth, women, SC/ST, and OBC entrepreneurs to start a new business.
eligibility: Permanent residents of Bihar, aged 18 to 50 years, with minimum qualification of Class 12 / Intermediate, ITI, Polytechnic, or Diploma. Proprietorship, partnership, or private limited company registration required.
salary: Up to ₹10 Lakh (₹5 Lakh interest-free loan, ₹5 Lakh grant/subsidy)
how_to_apply:
1. Apply online during the open registration window at udyami.bihar.gov.in.
2. Register using Aadhaar and mobile number.
3. Fill in details: educational qualifications, project type, and bank account.
4. Upload Aadhaar, Domicile certificate, Class 12 certificate, bank statement, and caste certificate.
5. Submit application for selection through computer lottery.
meta_title: Bihar Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana 2026: Apply Online & Form
meta_description: Apply online for Bihar Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana. Setup your startup with up to ₹10 Lakh support (50% subsidy, 50% interest-free loan). Check selection list.
content:
# Bihar Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana 2026

The **Bihar Mukhyamantri Udyami Yojana** is a major initiative by the Department of Industries, Bihar, to promote self-employment and small industries. It provides loans and grants of up to **₹10 Lakh** to start a new business.

### Scheme Categorization & Funds
- **Total Assistance**: Up to ₹10,000,000 (₹10 Lakh).
- **Subsidy/Grant Component**: 50% of the project cost (up to **₹5 Lakh**), which is completely free and does not need to be repaid.
- **Loan Component**: 50% of the project cost (up to **₹5 Lakh**), which is an interest-free loan (or 1% simple interest for general youth) repayable in **84 monthly installments** after a 1-year moratorium.

### Eligibility Criteria
- Must be a permanent resident of Bihar.
- Must belong to SC, ST, EBC, Women, or Youth categories.
- Must have passed Class 12th (Intermediate), ITI, Polytechnic, or equivalent diploma.
- Age must be between **18 and 50 years**.
- Must register a proprietorship firm, partnership, or Pvt Ltd company.

### List of Eligible Projects & Businesses
The scheme supports over 60 categories of manufacturing and service sector businesses, including:
- Food Processing (Flour mill, bakery, oil mill)
- Garment Manufacturing & Tailoring
- Concrete block & brick manufacturing
- IT/Computer center, printing press
- Automobile repair workshops

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar Card
- Bihar Resident Domicile Proof
- Educational Certificates (Class 10 and 12/ITI/Diploma)
- Caste Certificate
- PAN Card
- Cancelled Cheque or Bank Statement of Firm's Current Account
- Firm Registration details (GST, MSME Udyam, or Partnership Deed)

### Application Procedure
1. Visit the official website: [udyami.bihar.gov.in](https://udyami.bihar.gov.in/).
2. Click on the **Register** button during the active registration window.
3. Enter your Aadhaar number, Mobile number, and select the category.
4. Log in using the OTP sent to your phone.
5. Complete the application form, entering personal details, education, project details, and bank info.
6. Upload scanned PDF documents.
7. Submit the application. Selections are made via a computer-randomized lottery conducted by the department.

title: PM Vishwakarma Scheme 2026
department: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Central Government
overview: The Central Government operates the PM Vishwakarma Scheme to support traditional artisans and craftspeople (like carpenters, blacksmiths, potters, weavers, and tailors). The scheme offers skill training, ₹15,000 toolkit incentives, collateral-free loans up to ₹3 Lakh, and marketing support.
eligibility: Artisans or craftspeople engaged in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades in India. One member per family eligible; government employees and their families are excluded.
salary: Skill training stipend of ₹500/day, ₹15,000 toolkit grant, collateral-free loans up to ₹3 Lakh (at 5% interest rate), and marketing support.
how_to_apply:
1. Visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) for biometric-based registration on the official portal pmvishwakarma.gov.in.
2. Present Aadhaar card, mobile number, bank account details, and ration card.
3. Undergo biometric authentication and registration.
4. Applications go through a 3-tier verification: Gram Panchayat/ULB level, District Committee, and Screening Committee.
5. Get the PM Vishwakarma Digital ID Card and Certificate.
meta_title: PM Vishwakarma Scheme 2026: CSC Registration, Loans & Form
meta_description: Register for PM Vishwakarma Scheme 2026. Financial credit support up to ₹3 Lakh, ₹15,000 toolkit voucher, training stipend of ₹500/day. Check criteria.
content:
# PM Vishwakarma Scheme 2026

The **PM Vishwakarma Scheme** is a central government program designed to support, upskill, and provide credit to traditional artisans and craftsmen who work with their hands and tools.

### 18 Eligible Traditional Trades
The scheme covers 18 traditional occupations:
1. Carpenter (Suthar)
2. Boat Maker
3. Armourer
4. Blacksmith (Lohar)
5. Hammer and Tool Kit Maker
6. Locksmith
7. Goldsmith (Sonar)
8. Potter (Kumhaar)
9. Sculptor (Moortikar), Stone carver
10. Cobbler (Charmakar)/Shoesmith
11. Mason (Rajmistri)
12. Basket/Mat/Broom Maker/Coir Weaver
13. Doll & Toy Maker (Traditional)
14. Barber (Naee)
15. Garland Maker (Malakaar)
16. Washerman (Dhobi)
17. Tailor (Darzi)
18. Fishing Net Maker

### Scheme Benefits
- **Recognition**: PM Vishwakarma Certificate and ID Card.
- **Skill Upgradation**:
  - Basic Training (5-7 days) & Advanced Training (15 days or more).
  - Training stipend of **₹500 per day**.
- **Toolkit Incentive**: A grant of **₹15,000** to purchase modern tools.
- **Credit Support (Loans)**:
  - Collateral-free loans up to **₹3 Lakh** at a concessional interest rate of **5%**.
  - **First Tranche**: Up to ₹1 Lakh (18 months repayment).
  - **Second Tranche**: Up to ₹2 Lakh (30 months repayment).
- **Marketing Support**: Quality certification, brand promotion, and onboarding on e-commerce sites.

### Eligibility Criteria
- An artisan or craftsperson working in one of the 18 trades.
- Age must be **18 years** or above.
- Beneficiary must not have taken loans under similar credit schemes (like PMEGP, PM SVANidhi, Mudra) in the last 5 years.
- Only one member of a family (husband, wife, and unmarried children) is eligible.
- Government employees and their family members are strictly excluded.

### Registration Procedure
1. **CSC Visit**: Traditional artisans must visit their nearest **Common Service Centre (CSC)**. Self-registration is not allowed as biometric authentication is required.
2. **Biometric Registration**: The CSC operator will register the applicant on [pmvishwakarma.gov.in](https://www.pmvishwakarma.gov.in/) using biometric authentication (fingerprint/iris scan).
3. **Required Documents**: Bring your Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Bank Passbook, and active Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar.
4. **Verification**:
   - Level 1: Gram Panchayat (Pradhan) or ULB verification.
   - Level 2: District Implementation Committee.
   - Level 3: Screening Committee approval.
5. **Certificate Issue**: Once approved, the PM Vishwakarma Certificate and ID card will be issued, and the training and toolkit incentives will begin.

title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)

eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders and certain higher-income groups.
salary: Rs. 6,000 per year (Rs. 2,000 per installment)
how_to_apply: Visit the PM-KISAN portal (pmkisan.gov.in) and click on 'New Farmer Registration'. Enter Aadhaar number, bank details, and land records. Alternatively, approach local agriculture office or Common Service Centre (CSC).
meta_title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
department: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

eligibility: Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Low Income Groups (LIG), and Middle Income Groups (MIG). No pucca house in any part of India. Family income limits apply.
salary: Subsidy up to Rs. 2.67 lakh on home loan interest under CLSS; direct benefit of Rs. 1.5 lakh for EWS under BLC/ISSR.
how_to_apply: Visit pmay-urban.gov.in and click on 'Citizen Assessment'. Select your state, district, and city. Fill in personal details, income, and Aadhaar. Upload required documents and submit.

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
department: Ministry of Rural Development

salary: Rs. 1.20 lakh in plain areas; Rs. 1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas. Additional assistance for toilet and other amenities.
how_to_apply: Approach the Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office. Beneficiaries are selected from the SECC 2011 list. Application can be submitted through the PMAY-G portal by the local authorities.

how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch or post office. Fill the APY registration form. Provide Aadhaar, bank account details, and nominee information. The pension amount will be auto-debited from the account.

eligibility: Pregnant and lactating women aged 19+ for first two live births. Excludes women in regular government employment.
salary: Rs. 5,000 in three installments (Rs. 1,000 early pregnancy, Rs. 2,000 after 6 months, Rs. 2,000 after immunization).
how_to_apply: Visit the nearest Anganwadi center or health facility. Register with the PMMVY portal through the Anganwadi worker. Submit Aadhaar, MCP card, and bank details.

3. Provide Aadhaar and MCP card.
4. Receive installments after verification.

title: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
department: Ministry of Finance
overview: PMSBY is a one-year accidental insurance scheme renewable annually. It offers coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death or permanent total disability and Rs. 1 lakh for permanent partial disability. The premium is Rs. 20 per annum.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-70 years with a savings bank account. Aadhaar linked account preferred.
salary: Insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh for accidental death/disability.
how_to_apply: Visit your bank branch and fill the enrollment form. The premium will be auto-debited from your account. Alternatively, apply through the bank's internet banking or mobile app.
meta_title: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits

content:

- Low premium of Rs. 20 per year.
- Accidental death/disability cover.
- Auto-renewal.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar card
- Bank account details
- Mobile number

### How to Apply
1. Visit bank branch or use net banking.
2. Fill PMSBY enrollment form.
3. Provide Aadhaar and account details.
4. Premium deducted automatically.

title: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
department: Ministry of Finance
overview: PMJJBY is a life insurance scheme offering coverage of Rs. 2 lakh for death due to any reason. The premium is Rs. 436 per annum, auto-debited from the bank account. It is renewable annually.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-50 years with a savings bank account. Aadhaar linked account preferred.
salary: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakh.
how_to_apply: Visit your bank branch and fill the enrollment form. The premium will be auto-debited. Alternatively, apply through internet banking or mobile app.

title: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
department: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
overview: PMKVY is a skill certification scheme that provides free short-term training to youth, enabling them to secure jobs or become self-employed. Trainees receive a certificate and monetary reward upon assessment. The scheme covers various sectors.
eligibility: Indian youth aged 15-45 years. No minimum educational qualification required for most courses. Priority to school/college dropouts.
salary: Monetary reward of Rs. 8,000 (average) per trainee after certification.
how_to_apply: Visit the PMKVY portal (pmkvyofficial.org) or nearest training center. Register online or offline. Choose a course and complete training. After assessment, receive certificate and reward.
meta_title: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) 2026: Online Form, Eligibility & Benefits

1. Go to pmkvyofficial.org.
2. Click 'Apply Now'.
3. Register with Aadhaar and mobile.
4. Select course and training center.
5. Complete training and assessment.

title: Stand Up India Scheme
department: Ministry of Finance
overview: Stand Up India promotes entrepreneurship among SC/ST and women by providing bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore for greenfield enterprises. The scheme aims to facilitate at least two such projects per bank branch.
eligibility: SC/ST or women entrepreneurs. New enterprise (greenfield) in manufacturing, services, or trading. Borrower must be 18+.
salary: Loan of Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore.
how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch (public/private) and apply for Stand Up India loan. Submit business plan and required documents. The bank will process and sanction the loan.

- Loan from Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore.
- No collateral for loans up to Rs. 10 lakh.
- Refinance through SIDBI.

### Documents Required
- Identity proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID)
- Caste certificate (for SC/ST)
- Business plan
- Bank account details
- Project report

### How to Apply
1. Visit nearest bank branch.
2. Fill Stand Up India loan application.
3. Submit business plan and documents.
4. Bank verifies and sanctions loan.

title: Start Up India Initiative
department: Ministry of Commerce and Industry
overview: Start Up India is a flagship initiative to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups. It provides benefits like tax exemption, funding support, and easier compliance. Startups can register on the portal to avail benefits.
eligibility: Entity registered as a private limited company, LLP, or partnership firm. Turnover less than Rs. 100 crore in any previous year. Not older than 10 years from incorporation.
salary: Tax exemption for 3 consecutive years; funding support through Fund of Funds.
how_to_apply: Register on the Startup India portal (startupindia.gov.in). Submit incorporation certificate, pitch deck, and other documents. Get recognition number and avail benefits.

- Tax exemption under Section 80-IAC.
- Fund of Funds support.
- Easy compliance and self-certification.

### Documents Required
- Incorporation certificate
- Pitch deck
- Patent details (if any)
- Turnover proof
- Director details

### How to Apply
1. Go to startupindia.gov.in.
2. Click 'Register'.
3. Fill entity details and upload documents.
4. Submit for recognition.

title: Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
department: Ministry of Jal Shakti
overview: JJM aims to provide tap water connection to every rural household by 2024. The mission focuses on water supply infrastructure, source sustainability, and community participation. It also promotes greywater management.
eligibility: All rural households in India. Priority to households in water-scarce areas, SC/ST, and other vulnerable groups.
salary: Tap water connection provided free of cost to households.
how_to_apply: Approach the Gram Panchayat or Village Water and Sanitation Committee (VWSC). The scheme is implemented by the state government; households are identified through surveys.

- Improved health and sanitation.

### Documents Required
- Aadhaar card
- Residence proof
- Bank account details (for DBT if applicable)

### How to Apply
1. Contact Gram Panchayat or VWSC.
2. Register for tap connection.
3. Provide household details.
4. Connection provided after infrastructure setup.

title: PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
department: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
overview: PMGKAY is a food security scheme providing free food grains to poor households during the COVID-19 pandemic and beyond. It distributes 5 kg of wheat/rice per person per month, in addition to the regular PDS quota. The scheme has been extended multiple times.
eligibility: All beneficiaries under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), including Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) and Priority Households (PHH).
salary: 5 kg of food grains per person per month free of cost.
how_to_apply: No separate application required. Eligible households receive grains through their ration card at the local Fair Price Shop (FPS).

- Additional support during emergencies.
- Distributed through PDS.

### Documents Required
- Ration card (AAY or PHH)
- Aadhaar card (for authentication)

### How to Apply
1. No application needed.
2. Visit your designated Fair Price Shop.
3. Provide ration card and Aadhaar.
4. Receive free grains.

title: PM SVANidhi
department: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
overview: PM SVANidhi is a micro-credit scheme for street vendors affected by COVID-19. It provides collateral-free working capital loans up to Rs. 10,000 with subsidized interest and cashback incentives.
eligibility: Street vendors engaged in vending in urban areas as of March 24, 2020, with a valid certificate of vending or identity proof.
salary: Loan up to Rs. 10,000 with 7% interest subsidy and monthly cashback of Rs. 100-200 on timely repayment.
how_to_apply: Visit the official PM SVANidhi portal or nearest urban local body office. Submit application with identity and vending proof. Loan is disbursed through partner banks.

- 7% interest subsidy

- Incentives for timely repayment

### Documents Required
- Identity proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID)
- Vending certificate or letter from ULB
- Photograph
- Bank account details

### How to Apply
1. Visit the PM SVANidhi portal or ULB office.
2. Fill the application form.
3. Submit required documents.
4. Loan is sanctioned and disbursed to bank account.

title: Soil Health Card Scheme
department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
overview: The Soil Health Card Scheme provides farmers with a card containing soil nutrient status and fertilizer recommendations. It aims to promote balanced fertilizer use and improve soil health.
eligibility: All farmers in India are eligible. Soil samples are taken from their fields.
salary: Free soil testing and recommendations; no direct financial benefit.
how_to_apply: Farmers can contact the local agriculture department or visit the nearest soil testing lab. Soil samples are collected and tested, and the Soil Health Card is issued.

title: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana (DAY-NRLM)
department: Ministry of Rural Development
overview: DAY-NRLM is a poverty alleviation program that organizes rural poor into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides access to credit, skill training, and livelihood support.

salary: Revolving fund of Rs. 10,000-15,000 per SHG; community investment fund up to Rs. 5 lakh per SHG.
how_to_apply: Contact the Block Development Office or local SHG federation. Join an existing SHG or form a new one with 10-15 women.

title: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
overview: PMFBY is a crop insurance scheme that provides financial support to farmers in case of crop loss due to natural calamities. It offers affordable premiums and timely claim settlement.

salary: Premium: 2% for kharif, 1.5% for rabi, 5% for horticultural crops; balance subsidized by government.
how_to_apply: Loanee farmers are automatically enrolled. Non-loanee farmers can apply through banks, insurance companies, or online portal before the cutoff date.

title: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
department: Ministry of Labour and Employment
overview: PM-SYM is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers. It provides a monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after the age of 60 years.
eligibility: Unorganized workers aged 18-40 years with monthly income up to Rs. 15,000.
salary: Monthly pension of Rs. 3000 after 60 years of age.
how_to_apply: Visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or apply online through the PM-SYM portal. Provide Aadhaar, bank account details, and make the initial contribution.

- Family pension of 50% of the pension amount to spouse after beneficiary's death.
- Contribution amounts vary by age of entry.

### Documents Required

1. Visit a Common Service Centre (CSC) or the official PM-SYM portal.
2. Fill the enrollment form with personal and bank details.
3. Provide Aadhaar and mobile number for verification.
4. Make the initial contribution (cash at CSC or online).
5. Receive a registration number and contribution schedule.
